messages.author: swarm
messages.text:
BL-513 — Pipeline board LINKS section: list only the grid tickets, in plain alphabetical order (supersedes BL-506's most-recent-first)
What it solves: WHY A NEW TICKET, not a BL-506 amendment: the human's supersede decision arrived while BL-506
(LINKS most-recent-first, numeric-aware, all four sources) was ALREADY past the final QA gate and
integrating (QA-approved bcbae0426f, merge-up broadcast sent). An approved, integrating parcel
cannot be am…
Approval context: Carrying out your instruction to make the pipeline-board LINKS section list only the tickets shown on the board, in alphabetical order — superseding BL-506 (which had just shipped LINKS most-recent first across all link sources, so this reverses that ordering and drops the non-grid link sources). T…
BL-513 needs your approval before it can proceed. Reply here with "approve BL-513" (or "reject BL-513 <reason>") to act.

messages.author: swarm
messages.text: BL-513 🎵 in progress — Pipeline board LINKS section: list only the grid tickets, in plain alphabetical order (supersedes BL-506's most-recent-first)

messages.author: swarm
messages.text:
BL-513 — Pipeline board LINKS: link EVERY ticket shown on the board message (grid + parked + recently-closed + root-intake), MOST-RECENT-FIRST (highest ticket number), each link resolving to the ticket's CURRENT backlog folder — and re-post the pinned board when a link's path changes
What it solves: AMENDED IN-FLIGHT 2026-07-18 — TWICE. (a) 'grid-only' -> all-shown + current-folder paths + freshness.
(b) 'alphabetical' -> MOST-RECENT-FIRST: the human's third directive ('most recent tickets at the top')
reversed the alphabetical ordering, so this ticket NO LONGER changes the link order — it KEE…
Approval context: This RE-AMENDS BL-513 after your "most recent tickets at the top" directive. Ordering now stays MOST-RECENT-FIRST (highest ticket number first, BL-506's existing rule — the only deterministic "most recent" since the board carries no per-ticket timestamp); I dropped the alphabetical change entirely …
BL-513 needs your approval before it can proceed. Reply here with "approve BL-513" (or "reject BL-513 <reason>") to act.
